title: parahebe macrantha var. brachyphylla
intro: Parahebe macrantha var. brachyphylla, also known as a variety of the New Zealand hebe, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may provide health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues and wound healing. In herbal preparations, Parahebe macrantha var. brachyphylla is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
